items.title: What the jury will actually decide in the case of Elon Musk vs. Sam Altman
items.summary: Here's what the biggest tech court case of the year is all about.

items.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceXAI has been bleeding staff since its merger
items.summary: More than 50 employees have reportedly left Elon Musk’s newly merged SpaceXAI since February, raising questions about burnout, leadership changes, talent poaching, and whether liquidity events weakened retention incentives.

items.title: OpenAI says Codex is coming to your phone
items.summary: The update gives users enhanced flexibility over how they can manage their workflows.

items.title: Lovable just backed a company that’s looking to bring vibe coding to hardware
items.summary: Hardware company Atech raised $800,000 in pre-seed funding, including from a16z’s scout fund, Sequoia Scout Fund, and Nordic Makers.

items.title: Cerebras IPO makes billions for Benchmark but VC Eric Vishria almost didn’t take the meeting
items.summary: Benchmark almost never backs hardware startups. So Eric Vishria dragged his feet 10 years ago before agreeing to hear Cerebras' pitch.

items.title: What happens when AI starts building itself?
items.summary: Richard Socher's new $650 million startup wants to build an AI that can research and improve itself indefinitely — and he insists it will actually ship products.

items.title: OpenAI is reportedly preparing legal action against Apple; it wouldn’t be the first partner to feel burned
items.summary: OpenAI is so frustrated with Apple over a ChatGPT integration that failed to deliver the subscribers and prominence it expected that the company is now actively exploring legal action against the iPhone maker.

items.title: Clawdmeter turns your Claude Code usage stats into a tiny desktop dashboard
items.summary: A new open source gadget called Clawdmeter turns Claude Code usage stats into a tiny desktop dashboard for AI coding power users.

items.title: YouTube viewers watch 2 billion hours of Shorts on TVs each month
items.summary: Short-form video is built for mobile, so it may seem counterintuitive, but it's true: YouTube Shorts are becoming quite popular on the big screen.

items.title: Cerebras raises $5.5B, then stock pops $108%, in the first huge tech IPO of 2026
items.summary: A year ago, it looked like this day would never happen for Cerebras.

items.title: OpenAI says hackers stole some data after latest code security issue
items.summary: OpenAI said the damage was limited to the employees’ devices and did not affect user data nor its production systems, and none of its intellectual property was stolen.

items.title: Spotify to adopt Apple’s new video podcast tech, offering creators easier cross-platform distribution
items.summary: Spotify will let creators distribute and monetize video podcasts on Apple Podcasts using Apple’s HLS streaming technology, without changing their existing workflows.

items.title: Khosla Ventures is betting $10M on Ian Crosby, whose first startup, Bench, imploded
items.summary: Crosby is building Synthetic, a fully autonomous AI bookkeeping service for other startups.

items.title: How to turn off Instagram’s new Instants feature and retract photos you accidentally shared
items.summary: While the Meta-owned social network billed Instants as a new format to share real-life moments as they’re happening, many users are looking for a way  to turn off the feature, especially those who have accidentally sent images to others, not fully understanding how the feature works.

items.title: A spyware investigator exposed Russian government hackers trying to hijack Signal accounts
items.summary: A group of likely Russian government hackers tried to hack a security researcher who investigates spyware attacks. He was then able to turn the tables on the hackers and reveal details of their espionage campaign.

items.title: Cisco cuts nearly 4,000 jobs to spend more on AI, reports ‘record quarterly revenue’
items.summary: This is Cisco's latest layoff in recent years, while the company's chief executive touts record revenue and growth.

items.title: Wirestock raises $23M to supply creative multimodal data to AI labs
items.summary: The company pivoted to being a data provider in 2023 and now supplies datasets of images, videos, design assets, and gaming and 3D content to AI labs.

items.title: Two weeks left: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close May 27
items.summary: Your shot at VC access, global visibility, TechCrunch coverage, and $100K equity-free funding is running out. Deadline to apply is May 27. Apply now.

items.title: Uber to open 2 campuses in India to support product development, operations
items.summary: The expansion comes as Uber seeks new growth areas beyond ride-hailing and invests more heavily in AI, automation, and autonomous vehicle technologies.

items.title: Who decides what AI tells you? Campbell Brown, once Meta’s news chief, has thoughts
items.summary: "The conversation is sort of happening in Silicon Valley around one thing, and a totally different conversation is happening among consumers," Campbell Brown said at StrictlyVC.
